title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Recovery Case: Blame Context
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git blame context increment in a history-forensics evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
Investigate path history, content movement, follow heuristics, and limitations without assuming Git stores rename events.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Blame Context
steps.instruction:
theory case — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Blame Context contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Blame Context condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: githooks Manual — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

hints: Name the Blame Context precondition or invariant that the theory case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The author shown by blame necessarily introduced the current bug.

options.text: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Blame Context
steps.instruction:
theory case — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee6; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Blame Context application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

content.text: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. A defensible solution applies Blame Context, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-forensics evidence packet.

content.code:
git log --graph --decorate --oneline --all
git show --stat --summary HEAD
git cat-file -t HEAD^{tree}
git log --follow -- docs/notes.txt
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The author shown by blame necessarily introduced the current bug. This fails the Blame Context contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: githooks Manual (current as of 2026-07) — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

steps.title: Sequence Blame Context evidence
steps.instruction: Order the Blame Context evidence cycle for this theory task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Blame Context assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Build Blame Context evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-blame-context-1hppbs" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Blame Context into observable performance evidence.

content.code:

# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

steps.title: Defend the Blame Context model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Blame Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee6; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
